# Joshua Opolko

> GEO/AEO practitioner and self-hosted AI tool builder based in Toronto. First-party data on AI citation patterns. Founder of NowServingTO.

## GEO and AI Citation

- [GEO Field Manual](https://joshuaopolko.com/geo-field-manual/): Complete 10-step playbook for getting cited by ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, and Gemini. Includes first-party data and copy-paste prompts.
- [GEO Answers](https://joshuaopolko.com/geo-answers/): Practitioner Q&A on AI citation, updated weekly from real questions. Answer-first, falsifiable, grounded in first-party results.
- [GEO: AI Citation](https://joshuaopolko.com/geo-ai-citation/): Technical breakdown of how AI systems select citations, including the 134-167 word extraction window and passage-level optimization.
- [GEO Observatory](https://joshuaopolko.com/geo-observatory/): Live AI-crawler data for this domain. Which bots fetch which pages, fetch frequency, and freshness signals.
- [AI Crawler Report June 2026](https://joshuaopolko.com/ai-crawler-report/): First-party crawler data for June 13-27, 2026. 4,288 AI fetches, 20 bots, top pages, verification rates, and analysis.
- [Site as AI Infrastructure](https://joshuaopolko.com/ai-infrastructure/): How to structure a website as LLM-readable infrastructure for maximum AI citation.
- [LLM-as-ETL](https://joshuaopolko.com/llm-etl-architecture/): Using LLMs as extract-transform-load pipelines for structured data work.

## Self-Hosted AI Tools

- [Ollama](https://joshuaopolko.com/ollama/): Run local LLMs with Ollama on Mac, Linux, and Windows. Install guide and model management.
- [Qwen2.5-VL](https://joshuaopolko.com/qwen2-5-vl/): Local vision-language model that reads license plates, serial numbers, and documents from images through pure visual inference. OCRBench 864 vs GPT-4o-mini 785. Runs on a gaming GPU via Ollama.
- [Open WebUI](https://joshuaopolko.com/open-webui-self-hosted-guide/): Self-hosted ChatGPT-style interface for Ollama and cloud APIs. Install guide, RAG setup, and multi-user configuration.
- [AnythingLLM](https://joshuaopolko.com/anythingllm-self-hosted-guide/): Self-hosted AI workspace with RAG, agents, and multi-user support. Complete Docker setup guide.
- [LibreChat](https://joshuaopolko.com/librechat-self-hosted-guide/): Self-hosted multi-provider AI chat. Docker Compose setup, Ollama integration, and user management.
- [LiteLLM](https://joshuaopolko.com/litellm-proxy-guide/): OpenAI-compatible proxy for 100-plus LLM providers. Load balancing, fallbacks, and virtual key management.
- [n8n](https://joshuaopolko.com/n8n-self-hosted-guide/): Self-hosted workflow automation with AI nodes. Docker setup and AI workflow examples.
- [Dify](https://joshuaopolko.com/dify-self-hosted-guide/): Self-hosted LLM app development platform. RAG pipelines, agents, and API publishing.
- [Vane (Perplexica)](https://joshuaopolko.com/perplexica-self-hosted-guide/): Self-hosted AI search engine. Docker setup and SearXNG integration.
- [SearXNG](https://joshuaopolko.com/searxng-self-hosted-guide/): Self-hosted privacy-focused meta search engine. Docker setup and AI tool integration.
- [Agent Zero](https://joshuaopolko.com/agent-zero/): Self-hosted autonomous AI agent framework with persistent memory and tool use.
- [CrewAI](https://joshuaopolko.com/crewai-setup-production-guide/): Multi-agent AI framework for production workflows. Setup, role definition, and task orchestration.

## AI Workflows and Architecture

- [Building JOSIE](https://joshuaopolko.com/building-an-advanced-ai-workflow-josie-with-persistent-memory-and-live-data-access/): Advanced self-hosted AI workflow with persistent memory (Qdrant), web search (SearXNG), and n8n orchestration. Uses JOSIEFIED-Qwen3:8b via Ollama.
- [Claude Code Spec Workflow](https://joshuaopolko.com/claude-code-specification-workflow-mcp/): Specification-driven development workflow with Claude Code and MCP servers.

## Toronto Data Tools

- [Toronto Kids Events](https://joshuaopolko.com/kidsevents/): Free and low-cost kids and family events across Toronto, aggregated daily from EarlyON centres, libraries, parks, museums, and community groups.
- [Free Museum Days in Toronto](https://joshuaopolko.com/kidsevents/free-museum-days/): Every recurring free window at Toronto museums, including ROM, AGO, and Toronto History Museums.
- [HomeTurf Neighbourhood Guide](https://joshuaopolko.com/hometurf/): Compare all 158 Toronto neighbourhoods by household income, schools, childcare, demographics, and transit.
- [NowServingTO](https://nowservingto.com): Daily-fresh directory of Toronto's newest restaurants by cuisine.

## XR and Spatial Computing

- [Architectural Visualization](https://joshuaopolko.com/architectural-visualization/): WebXR architectural visualization with Three.js.
- [VR Therapy](https://joshuaopolko.com/vr-healing/): Virtual reality mental health applications and VR therapy research.
- [VR Elderly Care](https://joshuaopolko.com/revolutionizing-elderly-care-with-virtual-reality/): VR dementia therapy and aging care applications.
- [Vision and the Brain](https://joshuaopolko.com/vision-dominates-half-your-brains-processing-power/): How visual processing dominates human brain resources.

## About

- [About Joshua Opolko](https://joshuaopolko.com/about/): GEO practitioner and developer. Founded NowServingTO, a real-time restaurant wait-time tracker for Toronto.

## Contact

- GitHub: https://github.com/jopolko
